title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Mole Recipe (Pollo en Mole Sauce)
seo.metaDescription: Master the rich flavors of Mexican cuisine with our authentic Chicken Mole recipe. Learn how to make Pollo en Mole sauce from scratch for a delicious meal.
longTailKeywords: best Chicken Mole recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole)?
Ingredients for Authentic Chicken Mole Sauce
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Chicken Mole
Tips for the Best Homemade Chicken Mole
Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole
seo.imageAlt: A plate of authentic Mexican Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) with rice and cilantro garnish.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, complex flavors of this traditional Mexican Chicken Mole, a dish that's sure to impress!

description: Pollo en Mole is a quintessential Mexican dish, featuring tender chicken bathed in a rich, complex sauce that boasts a harmonious blend of chiles, spices, nuts, seeds, and a hint of chocolate. This deeply flavorful and aromatic sauce, known as mole poblano, offers a unique balance of sweet, savory, and subtly spicy notes, making every bite an unforgettable culinary journey. It's a celebratory meal that truly showcases the depth and tradition of Mexican gastronomy.

ingredients.notes: such as Ibarra or Abuelita, roughly chopped

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Place chicken pieces in a large pot, cover with water, and add a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and tender. Remove chicken and set aside. Strain and reserve 4 cups of the chicken broth.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chiles
instructions.text: Remove stems and seeds from the dried Ancho, Mulato, and Pasilla chiles. Heat a dry skillet over medium heat and briefly toast the chiles for about 30 seconds per side until fragrant, being careful not to burn them. Transfer the toasted chiles to a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them rehydrate for 20 minutes until soft. Drain and set aside.

instructions.title: Toast Aromatics and Spices
instructions.text: In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of lard or oil. Add the chopped onion, garlic, Roma tomatoes, and tomatillos. Cook until softened and slightly charred, about 5-7 minutes. Remove from skillet. In the same skillet, add the almonds, peanuts, sesame seeds, and the stale corn tortilla. Toast until golden and fragrant, about 3-5 minutes. Add the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, whole cloves, and black peppercorns, toasting for another minute until aromatic.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Combine the rehydrated chiles, the toasted aromatics (onion, garlic, tomatoes, tomatillos), and the toasted nuts, seeds, and spices in a blender. Add 1 cup of the reserved chicken broth. Blend until the mixture is very smooth, adding more broth if necessary to achieve a thick, pourable consistency. This may take several minutes.

instructions.title: Cook the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of lard or oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into the pot, pressing any solids through with a spoon. This step ensures a smooth mole. Stir continuously for 10-15 minutes, allowing the sauce to darken and thicken. It should be bubbling gently.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the Mexican chocolate until it is completely melted and incorporated into the sauce. Add salt to taste and the optional teaspoon of sugar to balance the flavors. If the mole is too thick, add more reserved chicken broth, a little at a time, until it reaches your desired consistency. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the mole sauce and simmer for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors and heat through. Serve hot with warm corn tortillas and Mexican rice.

tips: Toasting the chiles, nuts, seeds, and aromatics properly is crucial for developing the deep, complex flavors characteristic of an authentic mole sauce. Do not skip this step.
For the best flavor, use good quality Mexican chocolate, which often contains cinnamon. Brands like Ibarra or Abuelita are excellent choices. Adjust sugar to your preference.
Mole sauce can be made ahead of time and refrigerated for up to 3-4 days, or frozen for up to 3 months. The flavors often deepen and improve overnight.
article: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce is a beloved Mexican Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
